title: Lord, in Thy Spirit, take and fill my heart
firstLine: Lord, in Thy Spirit, take and fill my heart;
publicDomainBasis: George Croly (1780–1860), Frederick Cook Atkinson (1841–1896). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
Verse 2
sections.text:
I ask no dream, no prophet ecstasies,
No sudden rending of the veil of clay,
No angel visitant, no opening skies;
But take the dimness of my soul away.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Hast Thou not bid us love Thee, God and King?
All, all Thine own—soul, heart and strength and mind.
I see Thy cross—there teach my heart to cling.
Oh, let me seek Thee, and, oh, let me find.

sections.label: Verse 4
Verse 5
sections.text:
Teach me to love Thee with a virgin love,
One holy passion filling all my frame;
Thus all the riches of Thyself to prove,
My heart an altar, and Thy love the flame.
